```python
def shortest_palindrome(s):
    if not s:
        return ""
    
    # Create a modified string to find the longest palindromic prefix using KMP
    modified = s + '#' + s[::-1]
    lps = [0] * len(modified)
    
    for i in range(1, len(modified)):
        j = lps[i - 1]
        while j > 0 and modified[i] != modified[j]:
            j = lps[j - 1]
        if modified[i] == modified[j]:
            j += 1
        lps[i] = j
    
    # The length of the longest palindromic suffix
    longest_palindrome_suffix_length = lps[-1]
    
    # The part to prepend is the reverse of the non-palindromic prefix
    return s + s[:len(s) - longest_palindrome_suffix_length][::-1]

# Read input
import sys
s = sys.stdin.readline().strip()
# Compute and print the result
print(shortest_palindrome(s))
```
